title: Codex session bridge: single-live-session adoption + transcript_path persisted and used by recovery
action: skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/claims.mjs: resolveSessionId (flag -> BEE_SESSION_ID -> CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID) gains a durable fallback: when null, list live session records using the existing freshness predicate; exactly ONE fresh live session -> adopt its id and mark the resolution adopted (surface in the result object so call sites can audit); two or more fresh -> keep returning null so SESSION_REQUIRED still fires (real ambiguity refused). Callers in claimCellFile/claim-next/reservations paths pick this up automatically via resolveSessionId. Session-init hook (hooks source dir, bee-session-init.mjs): persist transcript_path from the hook payload into the session record via createSession. claims.createSession: accept and store transcript_path. skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/recovery.mjs + perf.mjs: when a session record carries transcript_path, use it directly for that session transcript resolution instead of the encoded-layout math; layout math stays as fallback. Tests: templates/tests/test_claims.mjs — sessionless call with exactly one fresh live session succeeds via adoption; with two fresh live sessions still refuses SESSION_REQUIRED; templates/tests/test_recovery.mjs — a session record with an explicit transcript_path at a NON-Claude-layout location (true Codex-shaped path) is resolved via the stored path.

truths: Sessionless mutation with exactly one fresh live session succeeds and is auditable as adopted
Two fresh live sessions still refuse SESSION_REQUIRED
A stored transcript_path at a non-Claude layout is used by recovery
prohibitions: Do not adopt when 2+ fresh sessions exist
Do not break the existing env/flag resolution order

verify: env -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u BEE_SESSION_ID node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_claims.mjs && env -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u BEE_SESSION_ID node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_recovery.mjs && env -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u BEE_SESSION_ID node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_state.mjs

trace.outcome: resolveSessionId gains a durable single-live-session adoption fallback (root-aware); claimCellFile adopts the sole fresh live session instead of refusing SESSION_REQUIRED, still refusing when 2+ are live; session-init hook + createSession persist transcript_path; recovery.mjs/perf.mjs resolveTranscript prefer the stored transcript_path over encoded-layout math, falling back when it is stale/missing.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": [
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_claims.mjs",
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_recovery.mjs",
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_state.mjs"
  ],
  "tests_added_or_changed": [
    "test_claims.mjs: replaced the single 'sessionless claim ... refuses SESSION_REQUIRED once another session goes live' row with three rows — solo (0 live) never adopted, EXACTLY ONE fresh live session auto-adopts (new, this is the RED row), TWO OR MORE fresh live sessions still refuses SESSION_REQUIRED (new)",
    "test_recovery.mjs: writeSessionRecord helper extended to accept transcript_path; two new checks — a session record with an explicit transcript_path at a non-Claude-layout (true Codex-shaped) location resolves via the stored path even with the Claude default root absent and no recovery.transcript_roots configured; a stale/missing stored transcript_path falls back to layout math exactly as before"
  ],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Before the fix, the new 'EXACTLY ONE fresh live session auto-adopts' row in test_claims.mjs FAILED: 'single-fresh-session adoption must succeed instead of refusing SESSION_REQUIRED, got {\"ok\":false,\"code\":\"SESSION_REQUIRED\",\"reason\":\"cell \\\"cell-single-adopt\\\" cannot be claimed without identifying the acting session while another session is active — pass --session-id or set BEE_SESSION_ID (CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID is also honored).\"}'. This is exactly the solo-session SESSION_REQUIRED bug D5 targets: claimCellFile(root, null, ...) with exactly one OTHER live session record (no CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID/BEE_SESSION_ID set, matching a native Codex session) was refused outright pre-fix (22 passed, 1 failed). Prior behavior (git show HEAD:skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/claims.mjs) had claimCellFile do only `if (session == null && isConcurrentMode(root)) return fail('SESSION_REQUIRED', ...)` with no adoption path at all, and resolveSessionId took only {flag} with no root-aware fallback.",
  "verification_run": "env -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u BEE_SESSION_ID node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_claims.mjs (23 passed, 0 failed) && env -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u BEE_SESSION_ID node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_recovery.mjs (37 passed, 0 failed) && env -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u BEE_SESSION_ID node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_state.mjs (31 passed, 0 failed) -> exit 0",
  "deliberate_exceptions": [
    "transcript_path is new surface (createSession/session-init hook never persisted it before this cell), so there is no prior-behavior 'before' to characterize beyond 'field was never written' — verified by reading the pre-change hooks/bee-session-init.mjs and claims.mjs createSession, neither referenced transcript_path at all.",
    "bee.mjs, cells.mjs, and reservations.mjs were deliberately left untouched (out of this cell's file list): claim-next's CLI-level `if (!sessionId) throw` guard in bee.mjs still requires an explicit session id ahead of claimCellFile, so the durable-fallback benefit reaches `cells claim` and any direct claimCellFile caller with session=null, but not the claim-next CLI path specifically — consistent with the cell's file scope."
  ]
}

PASS  createSession writes .bee/sessions/<id>.json (id, started_at, last_heartbeat); duplicate id is a typed failure
PASS  heartbeatSession advances last_heartbeat; missing session is a typed SESSION_MISSING failure
PASS  claimCellFile: first claimant wins, second gets typed CLAIMED naming holder and expiry — no throw
PASS  claim and session records are repo-relative — no absolute or system-temp path inside
PASS  isClaimActive reuses reservations TTL semantics: fresh claim active, TTL-expired claim inactive
PASS  sweep: TTL expired but heartbeat FRESH is never reclaimed (20260710 — no steal on a stall signal)
PASS  sweep: TTL expired AND heartbeat stale IS reclaimed; no gate file leaks
PASS  sweep and adopt skip/refuse while the per-claim gate is held — typed GATE_HELD, never wait
PASS  adoptClaim rewrites the owner in place: old owner loses, new owner holds, claim file present throughout post-state
PASS  releaseClaim: NOT_OWNER for the old session after adoption, owner release removes the file, NOT_FOUND after
PASS  claimCellFile default TTL matches the exported constant; released cell is claimable again
PASS  resolveSessionId: explicit flag wins over env; a blank flag falls through to env; neither present resolves null
PASS  resolveSessionId (hardening-4a): BEE_SESSION_ID wins over legacy CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID env; explicit flag still wins over both; a blank BEE_SESSION_ID falls through to the legacy env
PASS  claimCellFile(root, null, ...): a sessionless claim omits the "session" key entirely (never null) and still race-serializes via O_EXCL
PASS  releaseClaim(root, null, ...) releases a sessionless claim; a real session cannot release someone else's sessionless claim
PASS  clearClaim: unconditional removal regardless of owner; no-ops (ok:true, released:null) when there is no claim file; never leaks a gate file
PASS  isConcurrentMode: false with no session records or only the acting session live; true once ANOTHER session has a live heartbeat; a stale other-session heartbeat does not count
PASS  claimCellFile: sessionless claim still works solo (nobody else live) — byte-unchanged, never marked adopted
PASS  claimCellFile (hardening-1-7-10 D5 — Codex session bridge): a sessionless claim with EXACTLY ONE fresh live session auto-adopts that session's identity instead of refusing — this is the solo-session SESSION_REQUIRED bug the durable fallback fixes
PASS  claimCellFile (hardening-1-7-10 D5): a sessionless claim with TWO OR MORE fresh live sessions still refuses typed SESSION_REQUIRED — real ambiguity is never guessed, naming --session-id and BEE_SESSION_ID; a real session id still claims fine
PASS  race: claim-contention — concurrent workers racing one cell, exactly one O_EXCL winner every round
PASS  race: adoption-steal — a third session cannot steal a cell mid-adoption; every attempt loses with typed CLAIMED
PASS  race: sweep-heartbeat — concurrent sweepExpiredClaims + heartbeat renewal never reclaims a live claim (20260710)
